R1_flatshading.note: flatShading: true on all materials (sM, bookMats×5, pM). No change from C40. Consistent.

R2_zfighting.note: book.position.set uses bh/2 offset — books sit on plank surface. No coplanar overlap risk detected. +0.1 vs C40.

R3_materials.note: C40 fix: 15→5 shared bookMats reduces WebGL state changes. Disposal concern: bookMats are group-traversed on scene.remove(group) only if caller traverses and calls dispose(). Moon SpotLight still castShadow=false. +0.3 vs C40.
R3_materials.new_concern: MEDIUM — bookMats[b] disposal: LairDensity cleanup callback only removes moon.target, does NOT traverse group to call geometry/material .dispose(). If createLairDensity is called more than once (hot reload, run reset), the 5 BookMat + sM + pM instances accumulate in WebGL GPU memory. No evidence of multi-call pattern in MerlinLairScene but no guard either.

R4_lighting_dramaturgy.note: 5-source pass confirmed. Moon SpotLight (0xb8d4ff, 1.8, 18, penumbra 0.45) adds cold overhead counterpoint to key PointLight (0xff6618). HemiLight sky=0x2a3820 correctly Celtic-toned. No change from C40.

A5_glb_coverage.note: Procedural geometry only in LairDensity (shelves, books, parchments). GLB coverage unchanged from C40.

A6_proportions.note: Book dimensions (bw 0.30-0.44, bh 0.80-1.25) within human-scale lair. Parchment rolls (r=0.07, h=0.7) correctly small. No change.

A7_celtic_style.note: Color palette: dark wood shelf (0x3d2b1a), 5 book spines in earthy/jewel tones, parchment (0xd4b878). Moon-blue SpotLight (0xb8d4ff) matches Celtic moonbeam trope. No change.

P8_bundle_gzip.note: No new imports introduced in C40-C41 files. bookMats reduction removes 10 MeshStandardMaterial instantiations at runtime. Marginal positive impact on heap at startup.

P9_fps_budget.note: bookMats: 5 shared instances → 5 WebGL draw calls for 15 books (was up to 15 separate material contexts). State-change reduction is a genuine FPS improvement on low-end GPU. +0.1 vs C40.

P10_memory_leaks.note: mg_equilibre.ts: clearInterval on timerInterval — verified at line 137 setup assigns to this.timerInterval. No evidence of leak in mg_course. Moon target cleanup callback correctly returned. BookMats disposal gap noted under R3 but no confirmed multi-call path. -0.3 from ideal due to unguarded disposal.
P10_memory_leaks.new_concern: LOW — mg_equilibre setInterval (100ms) accesses DOM elements by ID. If container is removed before timeLeft reaches 0 (e.g., early game-over from parent), the setInterval keeps firing and calls document.getElementById which will return null. The null guards (if (statusEl)) and (if (fill)) protect against throws, but the interval is not cleared until endGame() or timeLeft <= 0. Parent must reliably call cleanup/destroy.

U11_aria_hotspot.note: C40 I-07 CLOSED: statusEl with aria-live='polite' added at lines 94-99. inZone field added at line 33, reset in setup() at line 123. setInterval at line 151 produces 'Equilibre: N% — En zone / Hors zone' every 100ms. mg_course keyboard guard closes I-08. +0.2 vs C40.
U11_aria_hotspot.focus_question_1_aria_spam: aria-live='polite' at 100ms update rate: polite means the AT queues the announcement and does NOT interrupt current speech. Screen readers typically batch-debounce polite live regions to ~500ms-2000ms depending on implementation (NVDA, VoiceOver, JAWS). A 100ms DOM text change with aria-live='polite' will NOT produce 10 announcements/second — the AT will coalesce to roughly one announcement per speech gap. This is the correct implementation pattern. VERDICT: NOT a spam issue. Sufficient AT feedback without flooding.
U11_aria_hotspot.residual_concern: LOW — aria-atomic is absent on statusEl. Without aria-atomic='true', some AT may read partial mutations (e.g., only 'En zone' if the text node update is not atomic). Adding aria-atomic='true' would guarantee the full 'Equilibre: 72% — En zone' string is always read as one unit.

G12_celtic_immersion.note: mg_equilibre lore text 'Marche sur le fil des druides' / 'Garde l'équilibre sur le Chemin d'Ogham' — strong Celtic register. 'En zone / Hors zone' text is neutral/functional, not lore-flavored. Minor immersion gap but functionally clear. mg_course timing guard does not affect lore.

I-07_mg_equilibre_aria_live.evidence: statusEl div with aria-live='polite' at lines 94-99. inZone field declared line 33, reset line 123. setInterval at line 151 produces correctly formatted text. See U11 focus analysis above.

I-08_mg_course_keyboard_guard.evidence: Line 80: if (this.roundElapsed < this.roundTime * 0.15) return; present. At tier 3 roundTime=0.8s, dead zone = 0.12s. See focus question 2 analysis below.

bookMats_shared_array.evidence: Line 15-17: 5 shared instances replacing 15 inline. State-change reduction confirmed. Disposal gap (cleanup callback does not traverse group) is a NEW residual concern — see R3.

Q1_mg_equilibre_aria_spam.rationale: aria-live='polite' does not cause announcements at the DOM update rate. Screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) coalesce rapid polite updates and announce at the end of the speech queue, typically yielding 0.5-2 announcements/second regardless of 100ms update frequency. The pattern is correct and idiomatic. The ONLY improvement available is adding aria-atomic='true' to guarantee full string atomicity.

Q2_mg_course_15pct_deadzone.verdict: ACCEPTABLE FOR TIER 3, BORDERLINE FOR TIER 1
Q2_mg_course_15pct_deadzone.rationale: Tier breakdown: tier0 roundTime=2.0s → dead zone=0.30s (15%). Tier1=1.4s → 0.21s. Tier2=1.0s → 0.15s. Tier3=0.8s → 0.12s. At tier 0 (casual), 300ms dead zone after round start is noticeable — a player who reacts quickly to a new target after ~250ms will silently miss. For pointer players this matters less (hitRadius gate), but keyboard players get a confusing silent rejection. The guard is well-motivated for tier 3 anti-mash but creates an asymmetric experience at tier 0. A tiered dead zone (e.g., roundTime * 0.10 at tier 0, * 0.15 at tier 3) would be more precise. SEVERITY: LOW — does not block PROCEED.

Q3_bookMats_disposal.rationale: The cleanup callback returned by createLairDensity() at line 47 only calls scene.remove(moon.target). The group (containing sM shelf meshes, 15 book meshes with bookMats, and pM parchment meshes) is added to scene with scene.add(group) at line 45 but the cleanup callback does NOT call scene.remove(group) nor traverse to call geometry.dispose() / material.dispose(). If MerlinLairScene's dispose() method correctly traverses the scene and disposes all children, this is safe. If it only calls scene.remove(group) without traversal, GPU memory accumulates. Needs verification in MerlinLairScene.dispose() (not read this cycle — token budget). Pattern risk is LOW given single-call usage confirmed in git history, but is an architectural gap.

new_issues.issue: cleanup callback (line 47) only removes moon.target — does not remove group from scene or dispose geometries/materials. If createLairDensity is ever called more than once (hot-reload, future run reset), 5 bookMat + sM + pM + all geometries accumulate in GPU memory.
new_issues.recommendation: Extend cleanup to: group.traverse(obj => { if (obj.geometry) obj.geometry.dispose(); if (obj.material) Array.isArray(obj.material) ? obj.material.forEach(m => m.dispose()) : obj.material.dispose(); }); scene.remove(group);

new_issues.issue: statusEl lacks aria-atomic='true'. Some AT (NVDA, older JAWS) may announce partial text updates ('En zone' without the percentage prefix) on rapid DOM mutations.

15% dead zone is appropriate for tier 3 (0.12s) but produces a 300ms silent rejection window at tier 0 (roundTime=2.0s). Keyboard players at tutorial tier may find legitimate fast reactions silently rejected.
new_issues.recommendation: Consider tiered guard: roundTime * (tier <= 1 ? 0.08 : 0.15). Keeps anti-mash at tier 2-3 while allowing faster response at tier 0-1.
